description: If the Context checks which concrete strategy it holds (instanceof, getClass, typeof), you've broken the pattern. The Context should be blissfully ignorant.
goodCode:
// Move ALL logic into the strategy itself
strategy.pay(amount); // Context just delegates
fix: Move the logic INTO the strategy. If the Context needs to behave differently per strategy, the strategy interface is too narrow -- add methods to it.

trap: YAGNI -- Strategy when you don't need it
description: Don't use Strategy when you only have ONE algorithm that won't change. Over-engineering with patterns is just as bad as under-engineering without them.
goodCode:
// Just call the method directly
GzipCompressor.compress(data);
fix: Ask: will this behavior ACTUALLY vary? If yes, Strategy. If not, just call the method. You can always refactor later.

trap: Fat Strategy -- SRP violation inside strategies
description: A strategy doing validation, logging, notifications, AND the algorithm violates SRP. Strategy does ONE thing -- the algorithm.
fix: Strategy does the algorithm. Use Decorator for cross-cutting concerns (logging, timing). Use Observer for notifications. Use a separate Validator.

description: Forgetting to set a strategy before calling the Context method causes a NullPointerException / segfault at runtime.
fix: Initialize with a Null Object default strategy. Never leave the strategy field as null.
